changes.value:
## What

Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes poor performance. The root cause is N+1 `DropZone` React components each with `onDragOver` handlers that call `setIsOver(true)` — triggering a React state update and re-render on every drag event (~50ms intervals). With 50 cards, that's 50+ components mounting event handlers and potentially re-rendering during drag.

### Current architecture
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` renders `tasks.map(...)` with a `DropZone` between each card plus one at the end (N+1 zones for N cards)
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — each `DropZone` has its own `useState(false)` for `isOver`, and `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` handlers that call `setIsOver`
- `onDragOver` fires continuously as the cursor moves — every call to `setIsOver(true)` when already `true` is a no-op at the React level (same value), but the event handler overhead across N+1 zones is still significant
- The bigger cost: each `DropZone` that transitions `isOver` from `false→true` re-renders with an expanded height (6px → 24px) and an animated indicator, causing layout reflow across all subsequent cards in the column

### Fix

Move hit-testing from per-zone React event handlers to a single column-level `onDragOver` handler that determines which zone the cursor is nearest to (by Y coordinate), and only updates one active zone index. This eliminates N+1 event handler registrations and reduces re-renders to at most 2 components per drag move (previous zone exits, new zone enters).

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — add a single `onDragOver` handler on the column container that computes the nearest drop zone by comparing `e.clientY` against card element positions (via `data-entity-card` attribute refs or `getBoundingClientRect`). Store the active zone index in a single `useState` or `useRef` + forceUpdate.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — change `isOver` from internal state to a prop (`active: boolean`). Remove `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` handlers. Keep `onDrop` handler.
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — update tests for prop-driven active state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is visually smooth (no jank)
- [ ] The correct drop zone still highlights as the cursor moves between cards
- [ ] Drop still works correctly — card lands at the indicated position
- [ ] Empty column drop zones still work
- [ ] Cross-column drag (drag from one column, drop in another) still works

## Tests
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — update for prop-driven `active` instead of internal `isOver` state
- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-drag-drop.test.tsx` — existing tests should still pass (zone computation unchanged)
- [ ] `npx vitest run` in `kanban-app/ui` — no regressions
- [ ] Manual: create a board with 50+ tasks in one column, drag a card over it — should be smooth
Perf: drag-over poor performance in columns with many cards

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,30 +1,27 @@
 ## What

-Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes poor performance. The root cause is N+1 `DropZone` React components each with `onDragOver` handlers that call `setIsOver(true)` — triggering a React state update and re-render on every drag event (~50ms intervals). With 50 cards, that's 50+ components mounting event handlers and potentially re-rendering during drag.
+Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes poor performance (jank/lag). The cause has not been profiled yet. Likely suspects:

-### Current architecture
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` renders `tasks.map(...)` with a `DropZone` between each card plus one at the end (N+1 zones for N cards)
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — each `DropZone` has its own `useState(false)` for `isOver`, and `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` handlers that call `setIsOver`
-- `onDragOver` fires continuously as the cursor moves — every call to `setIsOver(true)` when already `true` is a no-op at the React level (same value), but the event handler overhead across N+1 zones is still significant
-- The bigger cost: each `DropZone` that transitions `isOver` from `false→true` re-renders with an expanded height (6px → 24px) and an animated indicator, causing layout reflow across all subsequent cards in the column
+1. **Layout reflow** — when a `DropZone` transitions `isOver` from false→true, its height changes from 6px→24px, reflowing every card below it in the column
+2. **DOM node count** — N cards × (EntityCard + FocusScope + DropZone + field renders) = a large DOM tree that the browser must composite during drag
+3. **O(N²) claimWhen predicates** — `cardClaimPredicates` in `column-view.tsx` builds cross-column nav predicates for every card against every card in adjacent columns
+4. **React event handler overhead** — N+1 `onDragOver` handlers, though `setIsOver(true)` when already true is a no-op

-### Fix
+### Approach

-Move hit-testing from per-zone React event handlers to a single column-level `onDragOver` handler that determines which zone the cursor is nearest to (by Y coordinate), and only updates one active zone index. This eliminates N+1 event handler registrations and reduces re-renders to at most 2 components per drag move (previous zone exits, new zone enters).
+Profile first, fix second. Use Chrome DevTools Performance panel (or the `mcp__chrome-devtools__performance_start_trace` tool) to capture a trace of dragging over a 50+ card column. Identify the actual bottleneck before writing code.

-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — add a single `onDragOver` handler on the column container that computes the nearest drop zone by comparing `e.clientY` against card element positions (via `data-entity-card` attribute refs or `getBoundingClientRect`). Store the active zone index in a single `useState` or `useRef` + forceUpdate.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — change `isOver` from internal state to a prop (`active: boolean`). Remove `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` handlers. Keep `onDrop` handler.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — update tests for prop-driven active state
+### Files likely involved
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — per-zone isOver state + height animation
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — renders N cards + N+1 zones, computes claimWhen predicates
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — card render cost per item

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is visually smooth (no jank)
-- [ ] The correct drop zone still highlights as the cursor moves between cards
-- [ ] Drop still works correctly — card lands at the indicated position
-- [ ] Empty column drop zones still work
-- [ ] Cross-column drag (drag from one column, drop in another) still works
+- [ ] Profile captured and bottleneck identified
+- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is visually smooth after fix
+- [ ] Drop still works correctly
+- [ ] No regressions in drag-and-drop behavior

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — update for prop-driven `active` instead of internal `isOver` state
-- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-drag-drop.test.tsx` — existing tests should still pass (zone computation unchanged)
 - [ ] `npx vitest run` in `kanban-app/ui` — no regressions
 - [ ] Manual: create a board with 50+ tasks in one column, drag a card over it — should be smooth
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,27 +1,33 @@
 ## What

-Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes poor performance (jank/lag). The cause has not been profiled yet. Likely suspects:
+Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes jank because `DropZone` changes its height from 6px to 24px on hover (`style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}`). This triggers layout reflow of every card below the active zone in the column.

-1. **Layout reflow** — when a `DropZone` transitions `isOver` from false→true, its height changes from 6px→24px, reflowing every card below it in the column
-2. **DOM node count** — N cards × (EntityCard + FocusScope + DropZone + field renders) = a large DOM tree that the browser must composite during drag
-3. **O(N²) claimWhen predicates** — `cardClaimPredicates` in `column-view.tsx` builds cross-column nav predicates for every card against every card in adjacent columns
-4. **React event handler overhead** — N+1 `onDragOver` handlers, though `setIsOver(true)` when already true is a no-op
+The fix is simple: the drop zone should be a fixed height. The visual indicator (the blue bar) just toggles visibility — `opacity` or `visibility`, not height. No height change → no reflow → no layout shift.
+
+### Current (causes reflow)
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` line ~120:
+```tsx
+style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}
+```

-### Approach
+### Fix
+```tsx
+style={{ height: 6 }}
+// indicator inside uses opacity/visibility, not conditional render or height
+```

-Profile first, fix second. Use Chrome DevTools Performance panel (or the `mcp__chrome-devtools__performance_start_trace` tool) to capture a trace of dragging over a 50+ card column. Identify the actual bottleneck before writing code.
+The indicator bar is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.

-### Files likely involved
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — per-zone isOver state + height animation
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — renders N cards + N+1 zones, computes claimWhen predicates
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-card.tsx` — card render cost per item
+### File to modify
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove height toggle, make indicator always-rendered with opacity transition

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Profile captured and bottleneck identified
-- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is visually smooth after fix
+- [ ] Drop zone height does not change during drag-over (no layout reflow)
+- [ ] Visual indicator still appears when hovering over a zone
+- [ ] Dragging over a column with many cards is smooth
 - [ ] Drop still works correctly
-- [ ] No regressions in drag-and-drop behavior

 ## Tests
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — existing tests pass
 - [ ] `npx vitest run` in `kanban-app/ui` — no regressions
-- [ ] Manual: create a board with 50+ tasks in one column, drag a card over it — should be smooth
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: drag over 50+ card column — no jank, indicator still visible
\ No newline at end of file

-The fix is simple: the drop zone should be a fixed height. The visual indicator (the blue bar) just toggles visibility — `opacity` or `visibility`, not height. No height change → no reflow → no layout shift.
+The fix is simple: the drop zone should be a fixed height. The visual indicator just toggles visibility — `opacity` or `visibility`, not height. No height change → no reflow → no layout shift.

 ### Current (causes reflow)
 `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` line ~120:
@@ -16,7 +16,7 @@
 // indicator inside uses opacity/visibility, not conditional render or height
 ```

-The indicator bar is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.
+The indicator is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.

 ### File to modify
 - `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove height toggle, make indicator always-rendered with opacity transition

-The indicator is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.
+The indicator bar is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,33 +1,38 @@
 ## What

-Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes jank because `DropZone` changes its height from 6px to 24px on hover (`style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}`). This triggers layout reflow of every card below the active zone in the column.
+Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes jank from two compounding issues:
+1. `DropZone` changes height on hover (6px→24px), triggering layout reflow of every card below
+2. All N cards + N+1 drop zones are in the DOM regardless of visibility

-The fix is simple: the drop zone should be a fixed height. The visual indicator just toggles visibility — `opacity` or `visibility`, not height. No height change → no reflow → no layout shift.
+### Fix — two parts

-### Current (causes reflow)
-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` line ~120:
-```tsx
-style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}
-```
+**Part 1: Fixed-height drop zones (no reflow)**

-### Fix
-```tsx
-style={{ height: 6 }}
-// indicator inside uses opacity/visibility, not conditional render or height
-```
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove the height toggle (`height: isOver ? 24 : 6`). Drop zones are a fixed height. The visual indicator toggles visibility (`opacity`), not size. Each zone keeps its own `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` — no coordinate math, no column-level hit-testing. The zone knows when it's hovered and carries its own `DropZoneDescriptor`.

-The indicator is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.
+**Part 2: Virtualize the column list**

-### File to modify
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove height toggle, make indicator always-rendered with opacity transition
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — use `@tanstack/react-virtual` (`useVirtualizer`) to render only visible items. Each item in the virtual list is a (DropZone + Card) pair at a fixed height. The virtualizer manages the scroll container and only mounts DOM nodes for visible items + overscan.

+Since cards and zones are both fixed height, the virtualizer can compute positions exactly — no measurement needed. Drop zones retain their own drag event handlers and `DropZoneDescriptor` data, so drop behavior is unchanged. The virtualizer just controls which ones are in the DOM.
+
+This is a Tauri app (Chromium), so `@tanstack/react-virtual` works perfectly.
+
+### Files to modify
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — fixed height, opacity-based indicator
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — add `useVirtualizer` for the card+zone list
+- `kanban-app/ui/package.json` — add `@tanstack/react-virtual` dependency
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Drop zone height does not change during drag-over (no layout reflow)
-- [ ] Visual indicator still appears when hovering over a zone
-- [ ] Dragging over a column with many cards is smooth
-- [ ] Drop still works correctly
+- [ ] Drop zone height does not change during drag-over
+- [ ] Only visible cards + zones are in the DOM (check with DevTools)
+- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is smooth
+- [ ] Drop still works correctly — card lands at the indicated position
+- [ ] Drop zones still handle their own drag events (no column-level hit-testing)
+- [ ] Auto-scroll near column edges still works during drag

 ## Tests
 - [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.test.tsx` — existing tests pass
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-drag-drop.test.tsx` — zone computation unchanged
 - [ ] `npx vitest run` in `kanban-app/ui` — no regressions
-- [ ] Manual: drag over 50+ card column — no jank, indicator still visible
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] Manual: 50+ card column — smooth drag, correct drop positions, auto-scroll works
\ No newline at end of file

 ## What

-Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes jank from two compounding issues:
-1. `DropZone` changes height on hover (6px→24px), triggering layout reflow of every card below
-2. All N cards + N+1 drop zones are in the DOM regardless of visibility
+Dragging a card over a column with many tasks causes jank because `DropZone` changes its height from 6px to 24px on hover (`style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}`). This triggers layout reflow of every card below the active zone in the column.

-### Fix — two parts
-
-**Part 1: Fixed-height drop zones (no reflow)**
-
-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove the height toggle (`height: isOver ? 24 : 6`). Drop zones are a fixed height. The visual indicator toggles visibility (`opacity`), not size. Each zone keeps its own `onDragOver`/`onDragEnter`/`onDragLeave` — no coordinate math, no column-level hit-testing. The zone knows when it's hovered and carries its own `DropZoneDescriptor`.
-
-**Part 2: Virtualize the column list**
+The fix is simple: the drop zone should be a fixed height. The visual indicator just toggles visibility — `opacity` or `visibility`, not height. No height change → no reflow → no layout shift.

-`kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — use `@tanstack/react-virtual` (`useVirtualizer`) to render only visible items. Each item in the virtual list is a (DropZone + Card) pair at a fixed height. The virtualizer manages the scroll container and only mounts DOM nodes for visible items + overscan.
+### Current (causes reflow)
+`kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` line ~120:
+```tsx
+style={{ height: isOver ? 24 : 6 }}
+```

-Since cards and zones are both fixed height, the virtualizer can compute positions exactly — no measurement needed. Drop zones retain their own drag event handlers and `DropZoneDescriptor` data, so drop behavior is unchanged. The virtualizer just controls which ones are in the DOM.
+### Fix
+```tsx
+style={{ height: 6 }}
+// indicator inside uses opacity/visibility, not conditional render or height
+```

-This is a Tauri app (Chromium), so `@tanstack/react-virtual` works perfectly.
+The indicator is already absolutely positioned (`absolute inset-x-1 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2`), so it doesn't contribute to layout height. Just make it always rendered with `opacity-0`/`opacity-100` instead of conditionally rendered with `{isOver && ...}`.

-### Files to modify
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — fixed height, opacity-based indicator
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/column-view.tsx` — add `useVirtualizer` for the card+zone list
-- `kanban-app/ui/package.json` — add `@tanstack/react-virtual` dependency
+### File to modify
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/drop-zone.tsx` — remove height toggle, make indicator always-rendered with opacity transition

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Drop zone height does not change during drag-over
-- [ ] Only visible cards + zones are in the DOM (check with DevTools)
-- [ ] Dragging over a column with 50+ cards is smooth
-- [ ] Drop still works correctly — card lands at the indicated position
-- [ ] Drop zones still handle their own drag events (no column-level hit-testing)
-- [ ] Auto-scroll near column edges still works during drag
+- [ ] Drop zone height does not change during drag-over (no layout reflow)
+- [ ] Visual indicator still appears when hovering over a zone
+- [ ] Dragging over a column with many cards is smooth
+- [ ] Drop still works correctly
